rule_text: AP stores verification shall NEVER be entrusted to: (i) a low-paid subordinate, (ii) the custodian / ledger-keeper / accountant responsible for stores being verified, or to nominee/employee under them, (iii) anyone not conversant with classification, nomenclature, and connected technique of the stores; large-value/important-stores verification should be by a responsible officer INDEPENDENT of the executive officer in charge of stores.

AP library books may be purchased from reputed and standard book sellers on prevalent terms WITHOUT calling for tenders, subject to delegated financial powers of Head of Department; loss of 3 volumes per 1,000 issued/consulted in a year is reasonable; loss of any book > Rs.200 must invariably be investigated.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 144 Note
verification_method: For library-book procurement: tender process not mandatory; HOD financial-powers ceiling check + reputed-bookseller record

rule_text: AP store-verification deficiency investigations must be FINALISED WITHIN SIX MONTHS of detection; further extensions require Government order; controlling authority must prescribe time limits and ensure strict implementation.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 145 — GO Ms No 347 / Finance dated 1-12-1975

rule_text: AP Financial Code defines 'Works' broadly to include: construction and repair of buildings, roads, irrigation projects; manufacture, supply, carriage and repair of tools, plant, and stores required for works; generating stations, transmission and distribution lines, service connections — primarily classified as 'Original Works' OR 'Repairs and Maintenance' (with Repairs further classified as Ordinary Repairs OR Special Repairs).
verification_method: Foundational definition; treat all listed activities under AP Works rules
